title: Password Spraying Attack
story: SIEM correlation rule triggered: multiple accounts experiencing authentication
failures with low per-account frequency — a classic indicator of password spraying.
Unlike brute force attacks, password spraying attempts ONE or TWO common passwords
against MANY accounts to avoid account lockout policies.
Investigate the authentication logs and identify the attack pattern, source, and
whether any accounts were successfully compromised.

objectives.label: Count number of distinct accounts targeted
What is the failure sub_status code across all spray attempts?

hints: Start with: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case4_wineventlog event_id=4625 | head 20
Use: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case4_wineventlog event_id=4625 | stats dc(user) by src_ip | sort -dc
The sub_status field indicates why authentication failed — 0xC000006A means wrong password
Check for any 4624 (success) events from the same attacker IP
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case4_wineventlog event_id=4625 | stats dc(user) as targets, count by src_ip | sort -targets
index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case4_wineventlog event_id=4625 src_ip=10.0.0.200 | stats count by sub_status
